title: Medal of Honor Act
plain_summary: This law gives more money each month to people who won the Medal of Honor, which is the highest award for brave soldiers. Their monthly payment will go up from about $1,400 to over $8,000. The spouses of Medal of Honor winners who have died will also get about $1,400 each month. These payments will go up each year to keep up with rising prices.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Medal of Honor Act.

sections.title: Section 2  Findings
sections.text: Congress has found these facts. The Medal of Honor is the highest and most important military award in the United States. To earn the Medal of Honor, a person must do something so outstanding that it clearly shows their bravery went far beyond what was required of them. This goes beyond other forms of courage. The actions of Medal of Honor recipients inspire bravery and the willingness to give everything in those who serve in the military and those who will serve in the future. Those people whose names are on the Medal of Honor Roll show the best qualities of military members. They represent a long and proud line of people who went beyond what was required of them. According to section fifteen sixty two of title thirty eight of the United States Code, the Secretary of Veterans Affairs must pay a special monthly pension to each living person whose name has been entered on the Army, Navy, Air Force, and Coast Guard Medal of Honor Roll. Recipients of the Medal of Honor have earned a substantial and historic increase to this special pension. This increase recognizes their obvious gallantry, unwavering commitment, and heroic actions above and beyond the call of duty.

sections.title: Section 3  Increase in Department of Veterans Affairs Special Pension Payable to Medal of Honor Recipients
sections.text: Section fifteen sixty two of title thirty eight of the United States Code is changed in the following ways. In subsection (a)(1), the text is changed by adding the letter A in parentheses before the words The Secretary. The phrase the rate of one thousand four hundred six dollars and seventy three cents is removed and replaced with the rate described in subparagraph B. A new subparagraph B is added at the end that says the rate described in this subparagraph equals the amount of monthly compensation paid to a veteran without dependents under subsection m of section eleven fourteen of this title. This amount is increased to the next intermediate rate under subsection p of that same section. In subsection (e), the number one in parentheses is added before the word Effective. The phrase subject to paragraph two is inserted before the word shall. A new paragraph two is added at the end that says the Secretary shall not increase the amount of monthly special pension payable under subsection a in a year if that amount was already increased during that year under paragraph one.

sections.title: Section 4  Extension of Certain Limits on Payments of Pension
sections.text: Section fifty five oh three (d)(7) of title thirty eight of the United States Code is changed by removing the date November thirtieth, two thousand thirty one and replacing it with January thirty first, two thousand thirty three.
